content.text: The system shall allow the user to navigate to the Productivity Analytics screen through the navigation path: MHE → MEPS → Productivity Analytics, and upon selection the system shall load and display the Productivity Analytics dashboard.
navigate to the Productivity Analytics screen through MHE → MEPS
I can access the Productivity Analytics dashboard
the user is on the MHE module
the user clicks MEPS → Productivity Analytics
the system shall load and display the Productivity Analytics dashboard.
The system shall display a Productivity Analytics dashboard page accessible to authorized users.
access the Productivity Analytics dashboard based on my authorization
I can view relevant productivity data securely
the user accesses the Productivity Analytics screen
the system shall display the dashboard page.
The system shall display the page title “Productivity Analytics” at the top of the dashboard.
see the page title on the dashboard
I can confirm I am on the correct screen
the system shall show “Productivity Analytics” as the page title.
The system shall display navigation tabs including Task Productivity, Asset Productivity, Environment Productivity, and Trends & Insights.
I can navigate across different productivity insights
the user views the navigation section
the system shall display tabs: Task Productivity, Asset Productivity, Environment Productivity, and Trends & Insights.
The system shall allow the user to switch between Task Productivity, Asset Productivity, Environment Productivity, and Trends & Insights tabs.
I can view different types of productivity data
the user clicks on any tab
the system shall switch to the selected tab and display relevant data.
The system shall display Task Productivity tab as the default active tab when the Productivity Analytics page loads.
Task Productivity tab to be selected by default
I can immediately view task-related productivity insights
the system shall set Task Productivity as the default active tab.
The system shall display a KPI summary section at the top of the Task Productivity screen containing KPI cards.
I can quickly understand key productivity metrics
the Task Productivity tab is active
the system shall display a KPI summary section with KPI cards.
The system shall update KPI values dynamically based on the selected warehouse context and operational data.
I can view accurate data based on selected warehouse context
the warehouse context or data changes
the system processes updated operational data
the system shall update KPI values dynamically.
The system shall display the following KPI cards: Loaded vs Empty Travel %, Avg Pallets Moved/Hour, Idle-with-Load Time Lost, and Lost Hours Due to Aisle.
I can monitor key productivity indicators
the user views the KPI cards
the system shall display Loaded vs Empty Travel %, Avg Pallets Moved/Hour, Idle-with-Load Time Lost, and Lost Hours Due to Aisle.
Each KPI card shall display a title and corresponding value.
each KPI card to show title and value
I can clearly understand each metric
the user views any KPI card
the system shall display both title and corresponding value.

11.title: View Loaded vs Empty Travel KPI

content.text: The system shall display Loaded vs Empty Travel percentage values representing loaded travel and empty travel ratio.
see Loaded vs Empty Travel percentage
the system shall show loaded and empty travel percentage values.

12.title: Calculate Loaded vs Empty Travel KPI

content.text: The system shall calculate Loaded vs Empty Travel percentage based on movement telemetry data of MHE operations.
the system to calculate travel percentage accurately
I can rely on correct telemetry data
the system shall calculate Loaded vs Empty Travel percentage.

13.title: View Avg Pallets per Hour KPI

content.text: The system shall display Average Pallets Moved per Hour KPI with the unit pallets/hr.
see average pallets moved per hour
the system shall display value with unit pallets/hr.
The system shall calculate Average Pallets Moved per Hour based on completed pallet handling tasks during the selected time range.
accurate calculation of pallets per hour
pallet handling task data is available
the system shall compute average pallets moved per hour based on selected time range.
The system shall display Idle-with-Load Time Lost KPI in hours and minutes format (e.g., 0h 00m).
see idle-with-load time in readable format
I can easily understand time loss
the system shall display value in hours and minutes format (e.g., 0h 00m).
The system shall calculate Idle-with-Load Time Lost based on idle time recorded while MHE carries load without performing productive tasks.
the system shall calculate idle time when MHE carries load without productive tasks.

17.title: View Lost Hours Due to Aisle KPI

content.text: The system shall display Lost Hours Due to Aisle KPI representing productivity loss caused by aisle congestion or blockage.
see the Lost Hours Due to Aisle KPI
I can understand productivity loss caused by aisle congestion or blockage
the user views the KPI cards
the system shall display Lost Hours Due to Aisle KPI representing productivity loss due to aisle congestion or blockage.

18.title: Display Lost Hours in Hour Format

content.text: The system shall display Lost Hours Due to Aisle in hour format (e.g., 0.05 h).
see Lost Hours Due to Aisle in hour format
I can easily interpret the time loss value
the Lost Hours Due to Aisle KPI is displayed
the user views the KPI value
the system shall display the value in hour format (e.g., 0.05 h).

19.title: View Pallets Moved per Hour Chart Title

content.text: The system shall display a chart titled “Pallets Moved per Hour”.
I can understand what the chart represents
the system shall display the title “Pallets Moved per Hour”.
The system shall display a subtitle “Hourly Productivity Trend”.
see a subtitle for the chart
I can understand the context of the data
the user views the chart header
the system shall display the subtitle “Hourly Productivity Trend”.
The system shall display a line chart representing pallets moved per hour across the selected time range.
see a line chart of pallets moved per hour
I can analyze productivity trends over time
the system shall display a line chart representing pallets moved per hour across the selected time range.
The system shall display the X-axis labelled as “Time of Day”.
I can understand the time dimension
the system shall label it as “Time of Day”.
The system shall display the Y-axis labelled as “Pallets per Hour”.
the system shall label it as “Pallets per Hour”.
The system shall plot productivity values at defined time intervals across the selected time range.
productivity values to be plotted over time
the system shall plot productivity values at defined time intervals.
The system shall display data points for each time interval on the graph.
see data points on the chart
the system shall display data points for each time interval.
The system shall display a tooltip on hover showing time and pallets moved value.
I can view precise data values
the user hovers over a data point
the system shall display a tooltip showing time and pallets moved value.
The tooltip shall display time and corresponding pallet count (e.g., 11:00 – 4 pallets).
the tooltip to show formatted data
the user hovers on a data point
the system shall display time and corresponding pallet count (e.g., 11:00 – 4 pallets).
The system shall provide a Time Range dropdown filter (e.g., Today, Last 7 Days, Last 30 Days).
I can analyze data for specific periods
the user selects a time range (Today, Last 7 Days, Last 30 Days)
the system shall update the chart based on the selected time range.
The system shall provide a Session dropdown filter (e.g., All Sessions, Morning Session, Afternoon Session, Night Session).
I can analyze productivity for specific shifts
the user selects a session (All, Morning, Afternoon, Night)
the system shall update the chart based on the selected session.
The system shall provide an MHE selection dropdown (e.g., All MHE) allowing filtering by equipment.
I can analyze specific equipment performance
the user selects an MHE option
the system shall filter productivity analytics data accordingly.
The system shall display an MHE selection dropdown filter allowing users to select one or more MHE units to filter productivity analytics data.
select one or more MHE units
I can analyze combined or individual equipment performance
the user selects multiple MHE units
the system shall filter data based on selected MHE units.
The system shall display a search input field within the dropdown with placeholder text “Search MHE…” to allow users to search for specific MHE identifiers.
I can quickly find specific equipment
the user views the search field
the system shall display a search input with placeholder “Search MHE…”.
The system shall dynamically filter the MHE list based on the text entered in the search field.
the MHE list to filter dynamically
I can easily locate relevant MHE
the user enters text in the search field
the system shall dynamically filter the MHE list.
The system shall display MHE units grouped by equipment category (e.g., Aisle Master, VNA Truck, Stacker, Order Picker, Electric Pallet Jack, Electric Forklift).
I can easily navigate the list
the system shall group MHE units by equipment category.
The system shall display each equipment category as a group header within the dropdown list.
I can distinguish between equipment types
the grouped MHE list is displayed
the system shall display each equipment category as a group header.
The system shall display the MHE identifier under each equipment category (e.g., MHE-006, MHE-005, Order-Picker-1771492190980).
see MHE identifiers under each category
the system shall display MHE identifiers under each category.
The system shall display a checkbox next to each MHE identifier to allow selection or deselection.
select or deselect MHE using checkboxes
the user views each MHE item
the system shall display a checkbox to allow selection or deselection.
The system shall allow users to select multiple MHE units simultaneously using checkboxes.
select multiple MHE units using checkboxes
I can filter data for multiple equipment
the system shall allow selection of multiple MHE units simultaneously.
The system shall mark selected MHEs with a checked checkbox indicator.
the system shall display a checked checkbox indicator.
The system shall update the selected MHE list dynamically as users select or deselect items.
the selected MHE list to update dynamically
the user selects or deselects MHE units
the system shall update the selected MHE list dynamically.
The system shall display the total number of selected MHEs at the bottom of the dropdown (e.g., “10 of 10 selected”).
see the total number of selected MHEs
I can understand my selection count
the user views the dropdown footer
the system shall display total selected count (e.g., “10 of 10 selected”).
The system shall support scrollable dropdown behaviour when the number of MHE entries exceeds the visible area.
I can view all MHE entries
the MHE list exceeds visible area
the system shall support scrollable dropdown behaviour.
The system shall apply the selected MHEs as filters to update productivity analytics graphs and reports.
selected MHEs to filter analytics data
I can view relevant productivity insights
the system shall update productivity graphs and reports accordingly.
The system shall retain previously selected MHEs when the dropdown is reopened.
I don’t have to reselect them
the system shall retain previously selected MHEs.
The system shall close the dropdown when the user clicks outside the dropdown area.
the dropdown to close when I click outside
I can easily navigate the UI
the user clicks outside the dropdown
the system shall close the dropdown.
The system shall update the dropdown label dynamically to reflect the number of selected MHEs (e.g., “All MHE” or selected count).
the dropdown label to update dynamically
I can see selected MHE count
the system shall update the label (e.g., “All MHE” or selected count).
The system shall provide an Operator selection dropdown (e.g., All Operator) allowing filtering by operator.
I can filter data by operator
the system shall display an Operator dropdown.
The system shall display an Operator selection dropdown filter allowing users to filter productivity analytics data by operator.
the system shall filter productivity analytics data.
The system shall display a search input field within the dropdown with placeholder text “Search Operator…”.
a search field in the Operator dropdown
the system shall display a search field with placeholder “Search Operator…”.
The system shall allow users to enter text in the search field to locate specific operators.
enter text in the search field
the system shall allow input for searching operators.
The system shall filter the operator list dynamically based on the entered search text.
the operator list to filter dynamically
the system shall filter the operator list dynamically.
The system shall display an option “All Operator” at the top of the dropdown list to allow selection of all operators.
I can select all operators easily
the system shall display “All Operator” at the top.
The system shall display a list of operators available in the system below the “All Operator” option.
I can select from available options
the system shall display operators below “All Operator”.
The system shall display operator names as selectable entries in the dropdown list (e.g., Vikram Deshmukh, Amit Sharma, Rajesh Patil).
I can select the correct operator
the system shall display operator names as selectable items.
The system shall allow users to select an operator from the dropdown list to apply as a filter.
the system shall apply the operator filter.
The system shall update the analytics charts and reports based on the selected operator filter.
charts to update based on operator selection
the system shall update analytics charts and reports.
The system shall display the operator list in a scrollable panel when the number of operators exceeds the visible dropdown area.
the operator list to be scrollable
the operator list exceeds visible area
the system shall display a scrollable list.
The system shall retain the selected operator when the dropdown is reopened.
the system shall retain the selected operator.
The system shall close the dropdown when the user clicks outside the dropdown area.
the dropdown to close when I click outside
I can easily continue interacting with the page
the user clicks outside the dropdown area
the system shall close the dropdown.
The system shall update the dropdown label to reflect the selected operator or show “All Operator” when no specific operator is selected.
the dropdown label to reflect selected operator
I can see my current selection
an operator is selected or not selected
the system shall display selected operator name or “All Operator”.
The system shall display an empty state “No results for - ”  if no operators match the search input.
see a message when no operators match my search
I know no results are found
the user enters a search input
the system shall display “No results for-”.

62.title: Dynamic Chart Update Based on Filters

content.text: The system shall update the chart data dynamically based on selected filters.
charts to update dynamically based on filters
the system shall update chart data dynamically.
The system shall display a refresh icon/button to reload the chart data manually.
I can reload chart data manually
the user clicks the refresh icon/button
the system shall reload the chart data.
The system shall display a message such as “No data available / No pallets moved in this period” when productivity data is unavailable.
see a message when no data is available
I understand there is no productivity data
the system shall display “No data available / No pallets moved in this period”.
The system shall support vertical scrolling when dashboard content exceeds the visible screen height.
the dashboard content exceeds screen height
the system shall support vertical scrolling.
The system shall display a section titled “Lost Hours due to Congestion”.
the system shall display “Lost Hours due to Congestion”.
The system shall display a subtitle “Aisle Impact Analysis” below the section title.
see a subtitle for congestion analysis
the system shall display “Aisle Impact Analysis”.
The system shall display a bar chart representing lost operational hours caused by congestion in each aisle.
see a bar chart for congestion
I can analyze lost hours per aisle
the system shall display a bar chart representing lost operational hours per aisle.
The system shall display the X-axis labelled as “Aisles” showing aisle identifiers (e.g., Aisle A, Aisle B, Aisle C).
the system shall label it as “Aisles” with identifiers (e.g., Aisle A, Aisle B).
The system shall display the Y-axis labelled as “Lost Hours”.
the system shall label it as “Lost Hours”.
The system shall represent lost hours for each aisle using vertical bars.
the system shall display vertical bars for each aisle.
The system shall calculate lost hours based on operational delays caused by congestion events in aisles.
the system shall calculate lost hours based on operational delays.
The system shall display a tooltip when the user hovers over a bar showing the aisle identifier and corresponding lost hours value.
the user hovers over a bar
the system shall display tooltip with aisle and lost hours..
The tooltip shall display the aisle name and lost hours value (e.g., Aisle C – Lost Hours 0.04).
the user hovers on a bar
the system shall display aisle name and lost hours (e.g., Aisle C – Lost Hours 0.04)..
The system shall display a refresh icon allowing users to reload congestion data manually.
a refresh option for congestion data
the system shall reload congestion data.
The system shall update the chart dynamically based on selected time range, operator, session, or MHE filters applied in the dashboard.
congestion chart to update based on filters
filters (time range, operator, session, MHE) are applied
the system shall update the congestion chart dynamically.
The system shall display a section titled “Break Impact on Output Timeline”.
see the break impact section title
the system shall display “Break Impact on Output Timeline”.
The system shall display a subtitle “Productivity Drop Analysis”.
see a subtitle for break impact
the system shall display “Productivity Drop Analysis”.
The system shall display a time range indicator (e.g., “Last 7 days”) representing the period for which the data is displayed.
I know the period for which data is displayed
the system shall display the time range (e.g., “Last 7 days”).
The system shall display a combined chart visualizing break time and pallets moved over time.
see a combined chart for break time and pallets moved
the system shall display a combined chart visualizing break time and pallets moved.
The system shall represent Break Time using vertical bars.
break time to be shown as bars
I can easily identify break durations
the system shall represent Break Time using vertical bars.

82.title: View Pallets Moved as Line Graph

content.text: The system shall represent Pallets Moved using a line graph with plotted data points.
pallets moved to be shown as a line graph
I can track trends over time
the system shall represent Pallets Moved using a line graph with data points.
The system shall display a legend identifying chart elements including Break Time and Pallets Moved.
the system shall display a legend for Break Time and Pallets Moved.
The system shall display the X-axis showing date values for the selected period.
the system shall display date values for the selected period.
The system shall display the left Y-axis labelled as Break Time (hrs).
the user views the left Y-axis
the system shall label it as “Break Time (hrs)”.
The system shall display the right Y-axis labelled as Pallets Moved.
the user views the right Y-axis
the system shall label it as “Pallets Moved”.
The system shall display data points on the line graph representing pallets moved for each date.
see data points on the line graph
the system shall display data points for each date.

88.title: View Break Duration Bars per Date

content.text: The system shall display break duration for each date using vertical bars aligned with the corresponding date.
break duration mapped to each date
the system shall display vertical bars aligned to each date.
The system shall display a tooltip when the user hovers over chart elements.
the user hovers over chart elements
the system shall display a tooltip.
The tooltip shall display date, break duration (hours), and pallets moved values.
the system shall display date, break duration, and pallets moved.
The tooltip shall display additional contextual data such as total pallets moved and number of MHEs involved during that period.
the system shall display total pallets moved and number of MHEs involved.
The system shall display a refresh icon allowing users to reload break impact analysis data.
the system shall reload break impact analysis data.
The system shall display a section titled “MHE Task Productivity”.
the system shall display “MHE Task Productivity”.
The system shall display a subtitle “Equipment Utilization Performance” below the section title.
the system shall display “Equipment Utilization Performance”.
The system shall display a tabular report containing productivity performance metrics for each MHE.
the system shall display a table with productivity metrics.
The system shall display the following columns in the table: MHE, Utilization %, Pallet/Hr, Loaded %, Idle Load (min), Productivity Score, Trend.
the system shall display columns: MHE, Utilization %, Pallet/Hr, Loaded %, Idle Load (min), Productivity Score, Trend.
The system shall display the MHE identifier in the MHE column for each equipment unit (e.g., MHE-005, MHE-001).
the system shall display MHE identifiers (e.g., MHE-005).
The system shall display an equipment icon or identifier next to the MHE name to visually represent the equipment type.
I can visually identify equipment types
the system shall display an icon or identifier next to MHE name.
The system shall display Utilization % representing the percentage of time the equipment is actively used for operations.
the system shall display utilization % representing active usage time.

100.title: View Pallet per Hour for MHE

content.text: The system shall display Pallet/Hr representing the average number of pallets moved per hour by the MHE.
the MHE productivity table is displayed
the user views the Pallet/Hr column
the system shall display average pallets moved per hour.
The system shall display Loaded % representing the percentage of travel time when the equipment is carrying load.
the user views the Loaded % column
the system shall display percentage of travel time with load.
The system shall display Idle Load (min) representing the duration when the equipment remained idle while carrying load.
the system shall display idle duration while carrying load.
The system shall calculate and display a Productivity Score for each MHE based on predefined performance metrics.
the system to calculate productivity score
the system shall calculate Productivity Score based on predefined metrics.
The system shall display Productivity Score as a numeric value.
the system shall display numeric value.
The system shall visually represent Productivity Score using color-coded indicators (e.g., green for high performance, orange for moderate performance, red for low performance).
the system shall display color-coded indicators (green, orange, red).
The system shall display the Trend column indicating change in productivity performance compared to the previous measurement period.
I can track changes over time
the system shall display change compared to previous period.
The system shall display trend indicators using arrows and percentage values (e.g., upward arrow with percentage).
the system shall display arrows with percentage values.
The system shall display trend values as percentage change relative to the previous time period.
the system shall display percentage change from previous period.
The system shall provide a refresh icon allowing users to reload MHE task productivity data manually.
the system shall reload MHE productivity data.
The system shall support vertical scrolling when the number of MHE records exceeds the visible table area.
the system shall support vertical scrolling.
The system shall display an appropriate empty state message when no MHE productivity data is available.
a message when no data is available
the system shall display an empty state message.
The system shall display a section titled “Operator Task Productivity”.
the system shall display “Operator Task Productivity”.
The system shall display a subtitle “Individual Operator Performance” below the section title.
the system shall display “Individual Operator Performance”.
The system shall display a tabular report showing productivity performance metrics for individual operators.
the system shall display a table.
The system shall display the following columns in the table: Operator, Active %, Pallet/Hr, Loaded %, Idle Load (min), Productivity Score, Trend.
the system shall display columns: Operator, Active %, Pallet/Hr, Loaded %, Idle Load (min), Productivity Score, Trend.
The system shall display operator names in the Operator column.
the system shall display operator names.
The system shall display operator avatar icons containing initials of the Operator name (e.g., VD, KD, AS).
the system shall display avatar initials.
The system shall display Active % representing the percentage of time the operator is actively performing tasks.
the system shall display active working percentage.
The system shall display Pallet/Hr representing the average number of pallets handled by the operator per hour.
the system shall display average pallets handled per hour.
The system shall calculate Pallet/Hr using completed pallet handling tasks recorded for the operator during the selected time period.
the system shall calculate Pallet/Hr based on completed tasks.
The system shall display Loaded % representing the percentage of operator movement time while handling loaded equipment.
the system shall display percentage of loaded movement time.
The system shall display Idle Load (min) representing the duration during which the operator remained idle while the equipment carried load.
the system shall display idle duration.
The system shall display the Productivity Score as a numeric value for each operator.
the system shall display numeric value.
The system shall display Productivity Score using color-coded indicators (e.g., green for high performance).
the system shall display color-coded indicators.
The system shall display the Trend column representing change in operator productivity compared to the previous measurement period.
the system shall display change vs previous period.
The system shall display trend indicators using directional arrows and percentage values (e.g., upward arrow with percentage change).
the system shall show arrows with percentage.
The system shall provide a refresh icon allowing users to reload operator productivity data manually.
the system shall reload operator data.
The system shall support pagination controls including rows per page selection (e.g., 10 rows per page).
the system shall allow rows per page selection.
The system shall display pagination indicators showing the current record range (e.g., “1–1 of 4”).
the system shall display record range (e.g., “1–1 of 4”).
The system shall provide navigation controls to move to the next or previous page of records.
The system shall support vertical scrolling when the number of operator records exceeds the visible table area.
the system shall support vertical scrolling.
The system shall display an appropriate empty state message when no operator productivity data is available.
I understand no data is available
the system shall display an empty state message.
